title: Scrub, Style Queries, Balance, Mix & Native Carousel
description: Five live UI drops: scroll-scrubbed sprite animation, CSS container style queries, self-balancing typography, animatable color-mix() buttons, and a native ::scroll-button() carousel.

drops.title: Scroll-Scrubbed Sprites
drops.description: Canvas-generated sprite strips scrubbed frame-by-frame by animation-timeline: scroll() and steps() as the page scrolls.
drops.concept: Three sprite sheets (a walk cycle, a spin loader, a digit odometer) are drawn to an offscreen canvas at load time, then scrubbed through their frames purely by CSS scroll-driven animation — no scroll event listener drives the motion itself, only a progress readout does.
drops.source: Codrops — 'A Practical Introduction to Scroll-Driven Animations with CSS scroll() and view()' + classic steps() sprite technique

interaction.mouse: Scroll the page to scrub every sprite in lockstep

drops.title: Container Style Queries
drops.description: Three independent theme zones where a container's own custom property cascades a full re-theme to only its descendants.
drops.concept: @container style(--mode: vivid|dark|minimal) lets each panel's descendant cards react to a custom property set on their own container — flipping one panel's switch never leaks into a sibling panel, demonstrating true style-query encapsulation without any JS re-render.
drops.source: MDN 'Using container size and style queries' + Modern CSS Daily 'container-style queries'

interaction.click: Switches only that panel's descendant cards

drops.title: Self-Balancing Type
drops.description: The same editable headline set in normal, balance, and pretty text-wrap modes side by side, plus an lh-unit quote mark that stays baseline-locked.
drops.concept: text-wrap: balance evens out line lengths for headlines while text-wrap: pretty avoids orphaned last words in body copy — both compared live against normal wrapping as you type or resize. A quote glyph sized with height:1lh stays perfectly aligned to the text baseline at any font size, no magic-number padding required.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — 'CSS text-wrap: pretty' + loke.dev 'A Subtle Logic for the Balanced Headline'

interaction.click: Click into any headline to edit — all three stay in sync
interaction.touch: Same sliders and editable text work via tap

drops.title: Mood-Mix Buttons
drops.description: Five buttons whose hover/press colors blend smoothly via an animatable color-mix() custom property.
drops.concept: Registering --mix with @property lets a color-mix() blend transition smoothly like any normal CSS color, instead of snapping between two pre-baked swatches. A control panel lets you retarget the blend color and compare interpolation quality across oklch, oklab, srgb, and hsl color spaces.
drops.source: EdgeCases — 'CSS color-mix() for Dynamic Theming' + MDN color-mix() reference

interaction.mouse: Hover and press each button to feel the blend; adjust the panel controls

drops.title: Native Scroll-Button Carousel
drops.description: A scroll-snap carousel using the new ::scroll-button() and ::scroll-marker pseudo-elements, with a real JS fallback.
drops.concept: CSS Overflow 5 lets the browser generate real, auto-disabling prev/next buttons and dot markers for a scroller with zero HTML buttons via ::scroll-button()/::scroll-marker. Since CSS.supports('selector(...))') only proves selector parsing and not that a button is actually painted, this drop always ships working fallback controls rather than trusting that check alone.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — 'Carousels with CSS' + MDN ::scroll-button() reference

interaction.mouse: Click the ‹ › buttons or the dots, or scroll/drag the strip directly
interaction.touch: Swipe the carousel or tap the controls
